user_query:
We need to rebuild our incident response and change management system after several outages and a near-miss on a compliance audit. Read all materials in input/ and synthesize a single, testable architecture that preserves what works and ethically fixes what’s broken.

Please produce a six-part framework with strictly numbered sections and the exact section titles below in output/arkos_blueprint.md:
1) Intent
2) Constraints
3) Blueprint
4) Failure modes
5) Refactor path
6) Receipts

Requirements for arkos_blueprint.md:
- In “Intent,” preserve user and business goals you infer from the fragments and roadmap (use the word "intent" and “preserve”).
- In “Constraints,” include ethics and security constraints derived from input/constraints.yaml and any audit requirements (use both words “ethics” and “security”).
- In “Blueprint,” define clear modules and interfaces (explicitly use the words “modules” and “interfaces”) and explain how they interact. Separate Observed vs Inferred vs Unknown facts.
- In “Failure modes,” list at least 5 concrete failure modes as bullet points.
- In “Refactor path,” outline a minimal sequence of steps to get from the current state to the target state.
- In “Receipts,” define verification checkpoints, and include the anchor label ARKOS_OMEGA_FRAME. Also include the exact LYGO-MINT SHA-256 hash from input/arkos_canon.json so we can verify authenticity.

Also write these additional files:
- output/risk_register.csv — columns: risk_id,description,impact,likelihood,mitigation,owner. Include at least 5 rows, and ensure at least one row explicitly mentions a security or ethics risk.
- output/refactor_plan.tsv — columns (tab-separated): step_id,description,effort,owner,dependencies. Include at least 5 steps in the minimal refactor sequence.
- output/verification_receipts.json — an array of objects with fields: checkpoint, method, evidence. Include at least 5 receipts, one of which must reference the same LYGO-MINT SHA-256 hash from input/arkos_canon.json. At least one receipt should cover a live-fire incident drill or rollback test.

Inputs to read:
- input/fragments.md — messy notes and fragments of our current process and tools.
- input/constraints.yaml — policy, ethics, security, and regulatory constraints.
- input/incidents.csv — recent incidents with timelines and contributing factors.
- input/roadmap.json — the next 2 quarters of planned changes.
- input/arkos_canon.json — contains the canonical SHA-256 to reference in your receipts.

Make sure all outputs are self-contained, actionable, and auditable. Keep to text files only and write all deliverables under output/ with the exact filenames above.
